meaning: adj.像虫的 虫一样蠕动的
examples: The thick, wormlike larva of certain beetles and other insects.
Some caterpillars grow into butterflies. Other wormlike wriggling things become moths.
Any of various minute aquatic animals of the phylum Gastrotricha, having a wormlike, ciliated body.
The worm turned, and in fact it is at wormlike speed that things have been going south in Japan for a dozen years.
